steps.title: Identify the behavioral shift electric lighting would cause
steps.content:
The researchers conclude that electric lighting in the late nineteenth century prompted a behavioral shift visible in how fiction references time.

The key prediction: if lighting enabled evening activity, novels published after its spread should contain significantly more references to late-night activities than earlier novels.

steps.title: Find the evidence matching this prediction
steps.content:
Answer D directly demonstrates the predicted shift: novels after 1880 contain significantly more references to activities after 10 p.m., confirming that electric lighting enabled the behavioral change toward evening activity.

The other options either ignore activity timing as evidence of behavioral shift, address the format of references rather than their content, or fail to connect with the lighting mechanism.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
